homepage.text_excerpt: SARAH CHAMPION MP News About Sarah Rotherham Contact Gallery Back News News Archive Back Biography Key Successes Roles APPGs FAQs PRIVACY Criminal Injuries Compensation Authority Campaign Adult Survivors of Childhood Sexual Abuse Back Rotherham Council Wards Useful Contacts News News News Archive About Sarah Biography Key Successes Roles APPGs FAQs PRIVACY Criminal Injuries Compensation Authority Campaign Adult Survivors of Childhood Sexual Abuse Rotherham Rotherham Council Wards Useful Contacts Contact Gallery Hello, thank you for visiting my website Being the MP for Rotherham, I am always looking for different ways to keep in touch and serve my constituents. I hope you find my website helpful and informative. If you live in the Rotherham constituency and have a problem or wish to raise an issue, please contact me. I am always keen to hear from you . Sarah Champion MP Dare2Care National Action Plan Sarah Champion MP Follow @sarahchampionmp YouTube Facebook Latest News: Featured Apr 27, 2026 Sarah Champion MP Sponsors “Gold Standard Bill” to Stop Fox Hunting Apr 27, 2026 Sarah Champion MP is one of 12 co-sponsors of the Hunting Act 2004 (Amendment) Bill, which has been presented to Parliament. Fox hunting has continued despite the 2004 Hunting Act under the guise of “trail hunting” - a smokescreen used to hunt foxes with hounds. Read More → Apr 27, 2026 Apr 17, 2026 MPs strengthen law on hate crimes following campaign backed by Sarah Champion Apr 17, 2026 The MP for Rotherham who backed a campaign for a new law to equalise hate crime laws for LGBT+ and disabled people has expressed her delight that the change she campaigned on for a decade has now been passed by MPs and will become law. She also welcomed the Government’s move to introduce a new crime of hate motivated by misogyny for the first time. Read More → Apr 17, 2026 Apr 17, 2026 Sarah Champion welcomes funding boost for Hopian in Rotherham to strengthen community cohesion Apr 17, 2026 - Hopian in Rotherham awarded government funding to help bring people together and bridge divides - Part of over £2 million investment supporting more than 200 grassroots organisations across England - Funding will help strengthen unity, trust and opportunity within local communities Sarah Champion, Member of Parliament for Rotherham has welcomed new government funding awarded to Hopian recognising its vital work in bringing people together and strengthening community ties locally. Read More → Apr 17, 2026 Apr 14, 2026 Labour delivers for next generation defence workers with £50m for Defence Technical Excellence Colleges, including at Rotherham College Apr 14, 2026 The Labour Government has announced 5 Defence Technical Excellence Colleges (DTECs) across England, supported by £50m in funding. This is a commitment that the Government made in the Defence Industrial Strategy, and is now delivering on. DTECs will be existing further education colleges, recognised for providing ‘technical excellence’ and high-quality defence-focused education. They will provide training and education in crucial skills required by the defence sector, for students aged 16+, giving young people and adults the opportunity to gain the skills necessary to work well-paid, highly-skilled jobs and careers in the defence sector. Read More → Apr 14, 2026 Image Home Powered by Squarespace

pages.text_excerpt: Contact — SARAH CHAMPION MP News About Sarah Rotherham Contact Gallery Back News News Archive Back Biography Key Successes Roles APPGs FAQs PRIVACY Criminal Injuries Compensation Authority Campaign Adult Survivors of Childhood Sexual Abuse Back Rotherham Council Wards Useful Contacts News News News Archive About Sarah Biography Key Successes Roles APPGs FAQs PRIVACY Criminal Injuries Compensation Authority Campaign Adult Survivors of Childhood Sexual Abuse Rotherham Rotherham Council Wards Useful Contacts Contact Gallery Contact Me There is a strict parliamentary protocol which dictates that MPs may only represent their constituents, so please check first by entering your postcode here . Please also include your address however you contact me. I would also encourage you to read the Frequently Asked Questions page , which contains information on my surgeries, among other things. Phone For Press and Parliamentary enquiries: 01709 331035 For constituency and casework enquiries: 01709 331035 Post Send a letter to me – Sarah Champion MP, House of Commons, London, SW1A 0AA Email Please complete the form below, or email sarah.champion.mp@parliament.uk Powered by Squarespace

This is a commitment that the Government made in the Defence Industrial Strategy, and is now delivering on. DTECs will be existing further education colleges, recognised for providing ‘technical excellence’ and high-quality defence-focused education. They will provide training and education in crucial skills required by the defence sector, for students aged 16+, giving young people and adults the opportunity to gain the skills necessary to work well-paid, highly-skilled jobs and careers in the defence sector. This fulfils a key promise made to my constituents and will hopefully be the fulfilment of the long struggle for justice of former mineworkers and the Orgreave Truth and Justice Campaign.’ Read More Alexander Guest March 26, 2026 New Best Start nursery comes to Rotherham · Major expansion of school-based nurseries to deliver cost of living support worth up to £8,000 a year, with more nursery places in Rotherham from September to help parents balance work and family life · Comes as Labour’s early years policies half the cost of childcare, saving parents £8,000 on average and bringing costs back to 2005 levels · Families in Rotherham to feel the benefit of accessible and affordable childcare, as part of Labour’s plan to cut the cost of living Read More Alexander Guest March 23, 2026 Older Powered by Squarespace

pages.title: Biography — SARAH CHAMPION MP
pages.text_excerpt: Biography — SARAH CHAMPION MP News About Sarah Rotherham Contact Gallery Back News News Archive Back Biography Key Successes Roles APPGs FAQs PRIVACY Criminal Injuries Compensation Authority Campaign Adult Survivors of Childhood Sexual Abuse Back Rotherham Council Wards Useful Contacts News News News Archive About Sarah Biography Key Successes Roles APPGs FAQs PRIVACY Criminal Injuries Compensation Authority Campaign Adult Survivors of Childhood Sexual Abuse Rotherham Rotherham Council Wards Useful Contacts Contact Gallery Biography On 29th November 2012, I was elected as the first female MP for Rotherham, with 46.3 per cent of the votes and a majority of 5,318. At the 2015 General Election I increased my majority to 8,446, and at the 2017 General Election I received 56.4 per cent of the total votes, increasing my majority to 11,387. At the 2019 General Election , I was re-elected with a majority of 3,121. I do not consider myself a career politician. In an interview with BBC Radio Sheffield on 30th November 2012, I said: “There are some people who from the moment they were born wanted to be a politician, whereas for me, since I started working, I’ve always been working with the community and I want to carry on doing that.” From 2015 to 2016, I served as Shadow Minister for Preventing Abuse and Domestic Violence as part of the Shadow Home Office team, and from 2016 to 2017, I was Shadow Secretary of State for Women and Equalities. In January 2020, I was elected as by colleagues across the House of Commons as the first female Chair of the International Development Select Committee. I was born in Essex in 1969 and moved to Northamptonshire in 1977. I went to a comprehensive school and graduated from the University of Sheffield with a BA in Psychology. I was the first in my family to get a degree, but could only afford to attend because I was fortunate enough to get a full grant – and there were no tuition fees. I graduated at a time of high unemployment so made a living running art and environment workshops in schools and libraries for Rotherham Borough Council. I then gained full-time employment running the Rotherham Arts Centre until, in 1994, I went to work for Ashfield District Council as their arts worker. In 1996, I became CEO of the Chinese Arts Centre in Manchester, which I ran until 2008. This was an international agency promoting contemporary arts and culture. I became heavily involved in promoting diversity and equality, including advising the Arts Council of England on this for eight years. In addition, I was a Trustee and Chair of many arts and community organisations, including a community print co-operative, Boojum Theatre Company, Creative Capital and Step Out Arts. In 2008, I decided that I wanted my career to have direct social benefit and I was fortunate to become the Chief Executive of Bluebell Wood Children’s Hospice in Rotherham. The Hospice supports terminally ill children and their families. It was a deep wrench to leave the Hospice to become an MP, but I am committed to creating a fairer and more just society for everyone and I believe Parliament is the most effective way to achieve that nationally. Powered by Squarespace

pages.title: Criminal Injuries Compensation Authority Campaign — SARAH CHAMPION MP
pages.text_excerpt: Criminal Injuries Compensation Authority Campaign — SARAH CHAMPION MP News About Sarah Rotherham Contact Gallery Back News News Archive Back Biography Key Successes Roles APPGs FAQs PRIVACY Criminal Injuries Compensation Authority Campaign Adult Survivors of Childhood Sexual Abuse Back Rotherham Council Wards Useful Contacts News News News Archive About Sarah Biography Key Successes Roles APPGs FAQs PRIVACY Criminal Injuries Compensation Authority Campaign Adult Survivors of Childhood Sexual Abuse Rotherham Rotherham Council Wards Useful Contacts Contact Gallery Criminal Injuries Compensation Authority Campaign Sarah first became involved in campaigning for changes to the Criminal Injuries Compensation Scheme and CICA following the refusal of claims for compensation for victims of child sexual exploitation on the grounds that they had consented to their own abuse. Sarah wrote to the Ministry of Justice outlining concerns about the scheme and CICA in July 17 (here) and received a response ( here ). She subsequently met with the then Secretary of State for Justice, David Liddington and received a response in writing ( here ). Sarah highlighted also these issues in an article in the Times, available here , and in Parliament here . Sarah also highlighted the denial of claims on the grounds of a claimant having received a criminal conviction and the so called ‘Same Roof Rule’, which denies compensation to claimants if they lived under the same roof as the perpetrator prior to 1979. A subsequent review of CICA’s guidelines resulted in changes aimed to prevent victims of abuse being denied compensation the grounds that they consented to their abuse. In April 2017, Sarah co-ordinated a joint letter to the MoJ highlighting broad concerns about the Scheme and CICA. The letter and the response received are available here and here . In July 2018, a Court of Appeal hearing found the Same Roof Rule to be ‘arbitrary and unfair’. In September 2018, as a result of Sarah’s campaign, the Government announced a review into the Criminal Injuries Compensation Scheme. It also announced that the Same Roof Rule was to be abolished. Sarah’s response to the announcement is here . During a debate in October 2018 on the Victims’ Strategy, Sarah again highlighted to issues with the Scheme and CICA and outlined necessary changes. Sarah’s speech is viewable here . During the debate, the Solicitor General stated that changes to the Same Roof Rule would be implemented ‘ as soon as is practicable ’. Powered by Squarespace
